gaps: FORK-CHOICE vs EVAL-HARNESS CONTRADICTION IN THE RESEARCH BASE (most important gap). Two of the three eval-methodology VERDICT/RAW tracks build the SP-1 cost-capture design on oh-my-pi's `packages/agent/src/run-collector.ts` (AgentRunCollector, aggregateAgentRunSummaries, AgentTelemetryConfig.costEstimator) — verified present at C:/Users/deyan/Projects/Misul Terminal/research/sources/oh-my-pi/packages/agent/src/run-collector.ts:34-96,213-407 with input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite/reasoning/estimatedUsd buckets already wired into the loop. I confirmed this telemetry stack does NOT exist in pi-mono (Glob for packages/agent/src/run-collector.ts in pi-mono returns nothing; Grep for run-collector|telemetry|AgentRunCollector|costEstimator in pi-mono/packages/agent/src returns no files). The DRAFT recommends forking MINIMAL PI (which it itself notes has 'no per-session aggregation') and §4 silently rebuilds cost capture from scratch on Pi primitives. The draft never reconciles that its eval-harness research foundation is an oh-my-pi asset the fork choice throws away. Either the run-collector should be added to the cherry-pick list in §1, or §4 must explicitly state the eval-harness telemetry is being reimplemented and estimate that cost. This is a load-bearing omission.

PACKAGES NEVER READ / NOT IN COVERAGE. The oh-my-pi package inventory (Glob packages/*/package.json) is 15 packages: agent, ai, catalog, coding-agent, collab-web, hashline, mnemopi, natives, snapcompact, stats, swarm-extension, tui, typescript-edit-benchmark, utils, wire. The RAW research and draft cover ~8. NEVER inspected: `collab-web`, `tui` (oh-my-pi's, distinct from Pi's), `utils`, and `natives`/package.json (the N-API wrapper package — distinct from crates/). `utils` is a likely shared dependency of the very packages recommended for cherry-pick (hashline, catalog, swarm) and its portability/Bun-coupling is unassessed — a cherry-pick that transitively pulls `@oh-my-pi/utils` could reintroduce Bun lock the draft claims to avoid.

DRAFT REFUSED TO DO ANY VERIFICATION WORK. The draft's preamble states 'I have all the research I need. This is a synthesis task... No further tool calls are warranted.' For a fork-target and seam-mapping decision this is a process gap: the draft inherited line-number citations and shape tables from the RAW tracks without re-checking. Spot-checks I ran mostly held (calculateCost models.ts:39-48, snapcompact SHAPES lines 248-266, isBetterRun runner.ts:1724, applyServiceTierPricing openai-responses.ts, harness compact() phase!=='idle' at agent-harness.ts:711), but the draft had no way to know that when it declined to verify. The accuracy is luck-of-inheritance, not diligence.

SEAM MAP (§2) IS PI-ONLY AND OMITS THE OH-MY-PI SEAMS THE CHERRY-PICKS REQUIRE. §2 maps seams in minimal Pi but never maps the integration seams on the oh-my-pi side that the cherry-picks depend on: hashline's BlockResolver seam (hashline/src/types.ts), swarm's runSubprocess seam (swarm/executor.ts), snapcompact's renderSnapcompactPng N-API dependency, and how Pi's `afterToolCall`/`tool_result` hook maps onto a vendored hashline edit tool. The draft asserts these are portable but provides no seam-level wiring plan, leaving the actual integration cost vague.

NO TOKENIZER / COUNTING STRATEGY DECIDED. Cost math in §4 depends on token counts, but Pi has no tokenizer (the TS-edit-benchmark uses a 4-chars/token estimate, confirmed by the RAW risk note and diffTokenStats at runner.ts:1665-1679). oh-my-pi has tiktoken in the Rust crate. The draft's QpD metric and 'improvements below ~5pp are noise' threshold are undermined if token counts are themselves ±25% from char-estimation. Open decisions §7 omits this entirely.

COMPACTION TRIGGER TOKEN-ESTIMATION FRAGILITY UNADDRESSED IN THE TRIGGER DESIGN. §2/§7.6 correctly note no auto-trigger exists, but the RAW agent-core risks flag that estimateContextTokens is a hybrid (last AssistantMessage.usage.totalTokens + char/4 for the tail) that 'may fire too early or too late.' The draft tells Misul to 'build the trigger (token-threshold from message_end usage)' without inheriting this known accuracy caveat — the trigger it specifies is built on the same fragile estimate.

PARALLEL TOOL EXECUTION ORDERING/ROLLBACK RISK NOT CARRIED INTO §2 SUBAGENT SEAM. RAW agent-core flags that parallel tool execution emits tool_execution_end in completion order but writes results in source order, and that a mid-batch failure commits earlier results with no rollback. The draft's §2 'Subagent fan-out' row promotes executionMode parallel as the fan-out seam without carrying this consistency/rollback hazard — directly relevant since subagents-as-tools run in exactly this path.

NO MIGRATION/SCHEMA-OWNERSHIP DECISION FOR SESSION JSONL. RAW coding-agent flags 'Session JSONL v3 format is undocumented as a formal stability contract' and the eval harness in §4 assumes Misul sessions 'will use the same JSONL format (being a Pi fork).' But the SP-1 design also wants oh-my-pi's stats parser (which expects oh-my-pi's SessionEntry shape, Bun.JSONL.parseChunk). If Misul forks Pi (not oh-my-pi), the JSONL schemas may diverge and the stats-parser reuse claim in §4 ('zero additional code') is unverified across the Pi/oh-my-pi schema gap.
unverifiedClaims: §4 claims folding cache reads into input 'overstates cost ~10×'. The code (typescript-edit-benchmark runner.ts:1678-1679) sums input+cacheRead+cacheWrite for cross-provider TOKEN comparability, NOT for cost. The benchmark does not compute cost at all (RAW confirms 'no dollar cost in benchmark output'). So 'overstates cost ~10×' is a derived inference (cacheRead is ~0.1× input price on Anthropic) that the draft states as if it were a property of the benchmark. It is directionally true for a naive cost calc but the '~10×' figure is unverified and conflates token-summing with cost-overstatement.

§1 'crates/ workspace (~55k lines)' and '50+ tree-sitter grammars' — these line/grammar counts are inherited verbatim from RAW without independent confirmation. I did not verify them and the draft did not either. Treat as approximate, not load-bearing.

§3 'Break-even at 2 reads (1h-TTL write = 2× input)' for prompt caching. The VERDICT supports break-even at 2+ hits for the 1h tier; but the draft elsewhere mixes 5-min (1.25× write) and 1h (2× write) tiers. The single 'break-even at 2 reads' line does not specify which TTL, and for the 5-min tier break-even is lower. Minor laundering of the tier distinction.

§3 hashline '20-50% output cut on large-file/small-edit' conservative estimate. This is the draft's own synthesized range; the VERDICT only supports 'directionally sound, mechanism replicable' and author-run 30-61%. The 20-50% conservative band is an unverified interpolation presented as a finding.

§6 'Aider: ping every 5 min to extend Anthropic's 5-min TTL'. This competitive-steal claim comes from the survey/aider docs, not from code I could inspect. The cache-keepalive mechanism is plausible (VERDICT marks Aider caching 'supported') but the specific 5-min ping cadence is a secondary-source claim not independently verified.

§5 'GitHub: 19–62% token cut across 109 production runs' presented as 'best-evidenced quality-lift.' The VERDICT confirms this is from an official GitHub engineering blog with run counts, but it is token-EFFICIENCY data (workflow automation), not coding-agent QUALITY-lift data; the draft files it under 'quality-lift instruction techniques' which slightly overstates its relevance to correctness/quality.

§1 claim that catalog has 'type-only dependency on pi-ai.' RAW says catalog 'depends on pi-ai for type imports (Api, Usage etc.)' — but oh-my-pi's catalog depends on oh-my-pi's pi-ai fork, whose Usage/compat types may have diverged from upstream Pi's. The draft assumes a clean type-only graft onto Pi's pi-ai without verifying the two Usage/compat shapes are compatible (catalog's OpenAICompat has 30+ fields vs Pi's compat interfaces).
contradictions: INTERNAL: §1 recommends forking minimal Pi and notes Pi has 'no per-session cost accumulation,' requiring Misul to 'build it.' But the entire SP-1 eval-cost-capture research (RAW eval track + VERDICTS) is grounded in oh-my-pi's AgentRunCollector telemetry as 'the cheapest path to cost capture: already wired into the agent loop.' The draft adopts the eval-methodology conclusions while discarding the oh-my-pi infrastructure they depend on, and §4 quietly switches to 'attach to the stream()/complete() result promise' (Pi) without acknowledging the eval research assumed oh-my-pi's run-collector. The fork decision and the eval-harness design are sourced from incompatible bases.

INTERNAL: §3 ranks snapcompact as 'overstated / needs own measurement' and §7.3 says 'LLM-summary stays the safe default,' yet §6 (competitive insights to steal) lists 'Structured handoff-document compaction (oh-my-pi)' as a near-zero-cost win without noting that the handoff-document and shake mechanisms are part of the same oh-my-pi compaction pipeline whose adoption requires either the Bun/Rust stack or a port. §6 presents oh-my-pi compaction wins as free-to-steal while §1/§3 treat oh-my-pi adoption as high-cost.

INTERNAL: §4 'Best-run selection: swap isBetterRun to prefer the cheapest successful run' and the QpD metric optimize for cheap-success. But §4's own 'a system that fails cheaply has high QpD, zero value' warning and the 'track quality and cost as independent time series' instruction cut against making cheapest-success the best-run selector. Selecting the cheapest passing run per task is fine; the draft conflates run-selection-within-a-passing-set with the QpD optimization target without separating them.

INTERNAL: §1 says hashline degrades 'gracefully to null if you skip tree-sitter block ops' (BlockResolver), implying near-zero Rust dependence — but the verified RAW seam note says the tree-sitter AST resolver lives in crates/pi-ast via pi-natives. The draft treats hashline as 'pure TS, no Rust' (true for parse/apply/recover) while the block-level ops (replace_block etc.) that are part of hashline's pitch DO need the Rust resolver. The draft elides that the most differentiated hashline ops are exactly the ones with the Rust dependency.

CROSS-TRACK: §3 lists 'Context engineering / aggressive pruning' as 'High' impl cost and SP-2+ (needs trained 0.6B skimmer), but §5 and §6 treat superseded-read pruning and shake as 'near-zero-cost wins' to adopt now. SWE-Pruner (the trained skimmer) and superseded-read pruning are different techniques; the draft mostly separates them but the §3 'High / SP-2+' label visually buckets all pruning together, contradicting the 'always-on superseded-read pruning' adopt-now framing in §5/§6.
recommendedFollowups: Resolve the fork-vs-telemetry contradiction explicitly: read oh-my-pi/packages/agent/src/run-collector.ts and telemetry.ts in full, assess whether AgentRunCollector/aggregateAgentRunSummaries can be cherry-picked onto minimal Pi (does it depend on oh-my-pi's diverged agent types or just on Usage?), and either add it to the §1 cherry-pick list or rewrite §4 to own the full from-scratch cost-aggregation build with an effort estimate.

Read the unexamined packages: oh-my-pi/packages/utils, natives/package.json, tui, and collab-web. Specifically determine whether the recommended cherry-picks (hashline, catalog, swarm DAG, UserMessageMetrics) transitively import @oh-my-pi/utils or @oh-my-pi/pi-natives, which would reintroduce the Bun/Rust lock §1 claims to avoid. Map the dependency closure of each cherry-pick before asserting portability.

Verify the catalog→pi-ai type compatibility across the fork boundary: diff oh-my-pi/packages/catalog/src/types.ts (OpenAICompat 30+ fields, Usage) against pi-mono/packages/ai/src/types.ts to confirm a vendored catalog actually type-checks against UPSTREAM Pi's pi-ai, not just oh-my-pi's fork. The §1 'type-only dependency, trivial' claim is currently unverified across the divergence.

Decide and document the tokenizer/counting strategy as a new Open Decision in §7: Pi has none, oh-my-pi has tiktoken-in-Rust, and the only fallback is char/4. Quantify how char/4 estimation error interacts with the §4 '5pp = noise' threshold and matched-pairs CI; an unreliable token count undermines the QpD denominator.

Read oh-my-pi/packages/agent/src/compaction/{pruning,shake,branch-summarization}.ts (RAW competitive-scan cites these) to confirm the §6 'near-zero-cost steals' (superseded-read pruning, shake pre-pass, handoff document) are separable TS with no Bun/Rust dependency — the draft asserts this without the package having been read in the fork-judgment track.

Map the oh-my-pi-side integration seams omitted from §2: BlockResolver (hashline/src/types.ts), runSubprocess (swarm/executor.ts), renderSnapcompactPng (pi-natives), and produce an explicit wiring plan showing how a vendored hashline edit tool attaches to Pi's afterToolCall/tool_result hook and AgentTool interface. Quantify the runSubprocess re-implementation cost the draft calls 'the only porting task.'

Verify the JSONL schema compatibility assumption underpinning §4's stats-parser reuse: compare pi-mono session JSONL SessionTreeEntry shape vs oh-my-pi's stats parser SessionEntry expectations, since the draft forks Pi but reuses oh-my-pi's stats DB/parser. Confirm whether 'zero additional code' reuse survives the schema gap, or whether a translation layer is needed.

Carry the inherited Pi agent-core risks into §2/§7 as first-class warnings: (a) parallel-tool ordering + no-rollback on mid-batch failure for the subagent seam; (b) compaction token-estimate fragility for the trigger Misul must build; (c) fork() materializing full history (O(N) writes) if branch-heavy eval isolation is used. These are in RAW but dropped from the synthesis.

Add a self-verification pass before finalizing: re-confirm every line-number citation in §2 (api-registry.ts:66, agent-loop.ts:226/283/451, agent-harness.ts:384/723, models.ts:39, types.ts:104/109/115/120) against current files. Spot-checks passed but the draft explicitly declined to verify, so a full pass should precede SP-0 spec sign-off.
